title: Water wheel
abstract: The water wheel comprises a cylindrical casing (1), mounted on a horizontal shaft of rotation (8), including in the periphery thereof orifices in which respective plungers (2) move featuring inward and outward radial displacement, forming floats when they emerge outwards through said orifices. In order to do so, said movement is attained upon sprockets (3) meshing onto toothed sections (2) of a disc (7) mounted on the actual shaft of rotation (8) of the wheel, each sprocket (3) being associated to the corresponding plunger (2) through a rod (4) being articulated at one end to such plunger (2) and at the other end and eccentrically to the respective sprocket (3).
claims:
Claims 1. - Water wheel, characterized in that it comprises a cylindrical casing (1 ) rotating with respect to a support horizontal shaft (8) by means of which said casing is arranged being submerged in a liquid, characterized in that on the annular surface of the casing (1 ) a plurality of orifices is provided wherein corresponding radial plungers (2) are arranged being linearly movable in one direction or the other so as to emerge from said casing (1 ) and form thrust floats for said casing (1 ) rotation because of the flotation effect; each of the plungers being provided so as to be associated with each of the ends of a rod (4) through a joint (5), whereas said rod is associated at the other end through an eccentric joint (6) to a sprocket (3), with the special feature of the sprockets (3) being placed over an imaginary circumference line in the casing (1 ) which is concentric with a fixed disc (7) where two toothed sections (9) are arranged being phase shifted to each other by an angle near 180Â°, establishing the simultaneous meshing of two of the sprockets (3) or sections generating a hiding and emerging movement of respective plungers associated to said sprockets during the continuous rotation of the cylindrical casing (1 ). 2. - Water wheel according to claim 1 , characterized in that the horizontal shaft (8) on which the cylindrical casing is mounted has a U configuration, with the horizontal cross section (8) thereof establishing the actual shaft of rotation, and the lateral vertical sections (8") thereof establishing fixing arms and anchoring of the assembly onto a floating platform on the liquid surface where said water wheel assembly is submerged. 3. - Water wheel, according to claim 1 , characterized in that the disc (7) comprises a perimetral guide (10) for protecting and maintaining the position of the sprockets (3) which at that moment are not meshed, in the circular path thereof during the rotation of the cylindrical casing (1 ). 4. - Water wheel according to the previous claims, characterized in that the cross section (8 " ) of the shaft (8) on which the cylindrical casing is mounted (1 ) comprises orifices (1 1 ) as elements for compensating the pressure exerted by the plungers (2) upon hiding and emerging with respect to the annular surface of the cylindrical casing (1 ) itself. 5.- Water wheel, according to the previous claims, characterized in that it includes sealing means in the elements being submerged in the water, so as to render the inner area of the 

 cylindrical casing (1 ) sealed. 6.- Water wheel, according to the previous claims, characterized in that the total weight of the wheel assembly corresponds to 98% of the weight of the volume it displaces.
